quantification.why: Most comparisons list raw revenue totals side by side (e.g. "Gadget led with $2,392.00... ahead of Widget at $2,118.94") rather than computed gaps or ratios, with only "about 65% of North's total" being an actual computed comparison.

specificity.why: Every recommendation names a specific product or region with its figure — "Focus promotion on Gadget... $2,392.00," "Review the West region, which at $1,414.82 reached about 65% of North's total," and "Revisit Sprocket pricing... only $1,130.50."

insight_density.why: The prose surfaces multiple non-obvious, actionable observations, notably the volume-vs-revenue mismatch ("Sprocket trailed... despite high unit counts") and the West/North gap ("reached about 65% of North's total").

quantification.why: Nearly every comparison carries a data figure (e.g., "Gadget led with $2,392.00... ahead of Widget at $2,118.94") and includes a computed ratio ("about 65% of North's total").

specificity.why: All three recommendations name specific products/regions with figures, e.g., "Revisit Sprocket pricing, since high unit volume yielded only $1,130.50 in revenue."

insight_density.why: It surfaces multiple actionable observations beyond totals, such as the volume-vs-revenue mismatch ("Sprocket trailed... despite high unit counts") and the West/North gap at 65%.

quantification.why: Most comparisons attach raw dollar figures rather than computed gaps/ratios (e.g. "Gadget led with $2,392.00... ahead of Widget at $2,118.94"), with only the "West... about 65% of North's total" being a genuinely computed figure.

specificity.why: Every recommendation names a specific product or region with its figure — "Focus promotion on Gadget... at $2,392.00," "Review the West region, which at $1,414.82," and "Revisit Sprocket pricing... only $1,130.50."

insight_density.why: It surfaces multiple actionable, non-obvious observations including the Sprocket price/volume outlier ("high unit volume yielded only $1,130.50") and the West-vs-North gap ("about 65% of North's total").
